title: Exa MCP password and API key printed in plaintext by claude_code role
description: Discovered during ansible-all-my-things-pjoh (T011a validation, branch 014-desktop-profile): roles/claude_code/tasks/install-addons.yml tasks 'Check if exa MCP server is already configured' (line 265) and 'Configure exa MCP server' (line 277) loop over desktop_users, whose items contain plaintext password and exa_api_key fields. Neither task sets no_log: true, so every ansible-playbook run that hits this role prints the full item dict — including the real password and exa_api_key — to the console/log via Ansible's default loop-item echo. Confirmed live: a configure-profile.yml run against host romulus printed both secrets in full in the terminal output. Fix: add no_log: true to both tasks.
close_reason: Fixed in 40a54bc (no_log: true on both exa MCP tasks); verified live on host romulus — both tasks now show (censored due to no_log) instead of plaintext password/exa_api_key. Secrets rotated by user.

title: Extract nerd font install from tmux role into dedicated role
description:
WHY: The tmux role installs an iconic Nerd Font gated by tmux_install_iconic_font (roles/tmux/defaults/main.yml). This second responsibility violates Principle II (single responsibility) and is the root cause of the desktop-profile conflict recorded in feat.create-destroy-vm FINDINGS.md item 2: legacy configure-linux-roles.yml sets tmux_install_iconic_font: true as a play-var, which the planned Phase 5a verbatim import would carry into a shared role list and silently apply to basic-profile hosts. A Nerd Font only renders where a desktop renders glyphs locally (XFCE/XRDP); it is dead weight on headless servers. Extracting it removes the flag, kills the conflict at the source, and aligns font ownership with the desktop profile.

WHAT:
- New role (e.g. nerd_font): defaults (font version + arch map), tasks from roles/tmux/tasks/install-font.yml, meta, molecule/default scenario (port today's roles/tmux/molecule/with-font as the default scenario).
- tmux role: remove install-font.yml; remove tmux_font_version and tmux_install_iconic_font from defaults/main.yml; remove the install-font import + when from tasks/main.yml; delete molecule/with-font; drop the font-absent assertions from molecule/default/verify.yml; fix meta/main.yml description.
- version-update mechanism: re-point the ryanoasis/nerd-fonts fetch/query/report/update wiring from the tmux defaults path to the new role defaults path (playbooks/update-versions/query-versions.yml + perform-updates.yml). github_repo stays ryanoasis/nerd-fonts; only file path + var name change.
- consumers: remove tmux_install_iconic_font: true from configure-linux-roles.yml; the desktop profile includes the new role instead of setting a flag.
- The boolean tmux_install_iconic_font is removed entirely: desktop includes the role, basic omits it (selection by profile/group, not a flag).

SEQUENCING: do this before the Phase 5a desktop role-import step so the verbatim import is conflict-free.
acceptance_criteria: tmux role has no font tasks/vars/scenario and its molecule default passes; new font role molecule default passes (font present + fc-cache rebuilt); version-update query+perform still track the nerd font (no version escapes tracking); no headless host installs the font while the desktop path still gets it; the eventual desktop role import carries no tmux_install_iconic_font override.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #37 (mtps-extract-nerd-font-role): nerd_font role extracted, tmux stripped, version-update wiring + docs/specs re-pointed, both molecule scenarios green and idempotent.

title: Synchronize Molecule test runs across concurrent agents with a lock file
description:
## Context
Confirmed twice during ansible-all-my-things-jqmx (tmux console-only fix):
a concurrent agent session running 'molecule test' for a different role
(claude_code) executed 'podman rm -f instance' and rebuilt its own container
also named 'instance' while another session's molecule scenario was using
that same container name. Every role's molecule/*/molecule.yml in this repo
hardcodes platforms: - name: instance, so the podman container namespace is
effectively a single shared global resource across all concurrent sessions
on one host, even when sessions are isolated via separate git worktrees.

This is a generalization of ansible-all-my-things-luon (already tracked,
non-blocking finding) into a concrete, prioritized fix.

## Proposed approach (from user)
Use a lock file to serialize Molecule runs across concurrent agents:
- The first agent to run tests creates a lock file.
- If lock-file creation fails (file already exists), another agent is
  currently running tests; the new agent must wait (poll/retry) rather than
  proceed.
- Tests may only run once the lock file is successfully created.
- After tests finish (success or failure), the lock file must be removed.
- Likely implementation: a small wrapper script (using `mkdir`-based or
  `flock`-based locking for atomicity), either as its own script or embedded
  into scripts/test-molecule-all.sh, and adopted by any other entry point
  that invokes `molecule test` directly (e.g. per-role manual runs, CI).

## Why P1
Confirmed twice to actively corrupt test runs (container killed mid-converge,
false failures) when two agents/sessions work in parallel on this repo at
the same time. This is the project's stated parallel-worktree workflow
(see AGENTS.md branch-naming and worktree guidance), so the collision is
expected to recur, not a rare edge case.
acceptance_criteria:
- A locking mechanism exists (script and/or wrapper) that prevents two
  concurrent `molecule test` invocations on this host from running at the
  same time, using a lock file created atomically (e.g. mkdir or flock) with
  wait/retry semantics for the second caller.
- The lock file is reliably removed after the test run completes, including
  on failure/interruption (trap-based cleanup or equivalent).
- scripts/test-molecule-all.sh (and any documented direct `molecule test`
  workflow in CONTRIBUTING.md or the molecule-testing skill) uses or
  documents the locking mechanism.
- Verified by manually running two molecule invocations concurrently and
  observing the second one wait rather than collide.
close_reason: Implemented mkdir-based lock wrapper (scripts/with-molecule-lock.sh), wired into test-molecule-all.sh and molecule-testing skill doc. Verified via 7-role full regression (0 regressions) and real two-role concurrency test (second waits, no collision).

title: setup-nodejs.yml: shell tasks lack changed_when, always report changed
description: playbooks/setup-nodejs.yml tasks 'Install LTS/Jod Node.js' (line 18) and 'Identify current Node.js version' (line 29) are shell: tasks with no changed_when, so they report changed=true on every run regardless of actual state (nvm install/use/alias and nvm current are effectively no-ops when the LTS version is already installed/active). Found during configure-basic-profile (010) Phase 4 acceptance test, US2 Scenario 1 (FR-020/SC-006) on a fresh tart VM (risa) - second run reported changed=3 instead of changed=0, 1 of which is 'Install LTS/Jod Node.js' and 1 is 'Identify current Node.js version'. Fix: add changed_when: false to 'Identify current Node.js version' (purely read-only). For 'Install LTS/Jod Node.js', register the result and set changed_when based on stdout/stderr indicating an actual install/alias change occurred (e.g. absence of 'is already installed' combined with an alias change), or restructure into separate idempotent steps. Pre-existing bug in setup-nodejs.yml, not introduced by 010 but blocks its SC-006.
close_reason: Fixed and merged: PR #24 (09j6), PR #26 (85t5), PR #25 (syit)

title: setup-users.yml: password_hash random salt breaks idempotency for user accounts
description: playbooks/setup-users.yml line 28, task 'Setup accounts ...': password: "{{ item.password | password_hash('sha512') }}" has no fixed salt. Ansible's password_hash filter generates a random salt each invocation when none given, so the hash differs every run -> the 'user' module always sees a 'changed' password and reports changed=true for every account (gandalf, galadriel) on every run, even with no actual change. Found during configure-basic-profile (010) Phase 4 acceptance test, US2 Scenario 1 (FR-020/SC-006) on a fresh tart VM (risa) - second run reported changed=3 instead of changed=0, 2 of which are this task's loop items. Fix: seed the salt deterministically, e.g. password_hash('sha512', 65534 | random(seed=item.name) | string), so the hash is stable across runs per user while still varying across users. Pre-existing bug in setup-users.yml, not introduced by 010 but blocks its SC-006.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/eudicy/ansible-all-my-things/pull/24
close_reason: Fixed and merged: PR #24 (09j6), PR #26 (85t5), PR #25 (syit)

title: setup-users.yml: PasswordAuthentication no overridden by cloud-init sshd_config.d drop-in
description: playbooks/setup-users.yml sets 'PasswordAuthentication no' via lineinfile on /etc/ssh/sshd_config. Ubuntu cloud images Include /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/*.conf near the top of sshd_config; 50-cloud-init.conf sets 'PasswordAuthentication yes' and is processed first (sshd is first-match-wins), so it wins over 60-cloudimg-settings.conf's 'no' and over our appended line. Effective sshd -T reports passwordauthentication yes despite the playbook's intent. Found during configure-basic-profile (010) Phase 4 acceptance test, Scenario 2 (FR-006/SC-002) on a fresh tart VM (risa). Fix: replace the lineinfile with a copy/template task writing /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/10-disable-password-auth.conf containing 'PasswordAuthentication no' (sorts before 50-cloud-init.conf, wins via first-match), keep the existing 'Restart SSH' handler notify. Pre-existing bug in setup-users.yml, not introduced by 010 but blocks its SC-002.
notes: PR opened: https://github.com/eudicy/ansible-all-my-things/pull/26
close_reason: Fixed and merged: PR #24 (09j6), PR #26 (85t5), PR #25 (syit)

title: Create beads molecule pr-review-cycle for PR review workflow
description:
## Why

AI-implemented features end with a PR to origin/main. A human reviews that PR and files review findings. All findings must be resolved before the PR can merge. Without a standard template, each agent session re-invents this workflow ad-hoc.

## What

Create a proto (template epic with label 'template') named 'pr-review-cycle'. Structure:

- Proto epic: `pr-review-cycle` (label: template)
  - Story: 'Review PR and collect findings' (child of proto)
    - Task: 'Merge feature branch to origin/main' (child of story)
    - [Finding tasks added dynamically, each blocking the merge task]

When an agent instantiates the molecule for a feature:
1. Pour the proto: `bd mol pour pr-review-cycle`
2. Attach the user story as child of the parent feature: `bd update <story-id> --parent <feature-id>`
3. For each human review finding: create a task child of the story, wire as dep blocking the merge task

## Acceptance criteria

- `bd formula list` or `bd mol show pr-review-cycle` shows the proto
- Proto contains story 'Review PR and collect findings'
- Story contains task 'Merge feature branch to origin/main'
- `bd mol pour pr-review-cycle` instantiates the full structure correctly
- AGENTS.md documents when and how to instantiate this molecule
- `bd mol pour pr-review-cycle` is successfully run on this very issue (y7sa): story attached as child of y7sa, merge task present and wired correctly — the molecule validates itself
close_reason: Closed

title: dolt-sql-server fails to start: missing HOME directory for dolt user
description: Service fails with 'Failed to load the HOME directory: stat /home/dolt: no such file or directory'. Dolt reads $HOME at startup. Service user created with create_home: false, so no /home/dolt exists. Fix: set Environment=HOME={{ dolt_data_dir }} and WorkingDirectory={{ dolt_data_dir }} in the systemd service unit template.
close_reason: Fixed: added Environment=HOME and WorkingDirectory to service unit template. Molecule tests pass.

title: Install Ruby on new VMs to support OMC ralph skill
description:
## Problem
Recent OMC version update introduced a breaking change: the ralph skill now requires Ruby to be installed. New VMs provisioned without Ruby cannot run ralph, breaking the OMC agent workflow.

## Goal
Ensure Ruby is installed as part of the VM provisioning process so ralph works out of the box on all new VMs.

## Scope
- Add Ruby installation to the hcloud-linux provisioner (provisioners/hcloud-linux.yml) or the relevant role
- Verify ralph skill is functional after provisioning

## Impact
Affects all new VMs. Existing VMs without Ruby cannot use ralph.
acceptance_criteria: Run 'omc doctor' on freshly provisioned VM — all checks must show OK, no WARN or CRITICAL
close_reason: Ruby role created (roles/ruby), apt install, Molecule verified, wired as explicit Ansible dependency in claude_code/meta/main.yml

title: Run Dolt SQL server locally so parallel agents can write to beads concurrently
description:
## Context

Agent VMs are ephemeral — destroyed after each session, recreated from scratch for the next. Beads data persists via git (`refs/dolt/data`), synchronized with `bd dolt push` at session end and restored with `bd bootstrap` (or `bd dolt pull`) at session start.

By default beads uses an embedded Dolt backend that permits only one concurrent writer. When multiple AI agents run in parallel via `my-omc-parallel-work`, all but one agent lose write access, causing lost task tracking and agent focus drift.

## Problem

Fresh VMs have no Dolt SQL server. Without one, parallel agents cannot write to beads concurrently.

## Goal

Create an Ansible role that installs Dolt and starts a Dolt SQL server as a systemd service on each new VM. All parallel agent sessions connect to this server instead of using embedded Dolt, eliminating write-lock contention.

## Scope

1. New Ansible role `roles/dolt_sql_server` with a Molecule test scenario
2. Install the Dolt binary (https://github.com/dolthub/dolt)
3. Configure and enable `dolt sql-server` as a systemd service (starts on every VM boot)
4. Update `.beads/config.yaml` to connect to the local Dolt SQL server
5. Add the role to `configure-linux-roles.yml` so it runs as part of base VM provisioning

## Role boundary

The role is responsible for: install + systemd service only.

Out of scope for the role:
- Database initialization (`dolt init`) — owned by `bd bootstrap`
- Data restore — owned by `bd dolt pull` / `bd bootstrap` at session start
- Data backup — owned by `bd dolt push` at session end

## Expected session lifecycle (after this feature)

1. VM created → provisioning runs `configure-linux-roles.yml` → Dolt SQL server starts
2. Agent session starts → `bd bootstrap` (or `bd dolt pull`) restores beads data from `refs/dolt/data`
3. Agents work, including parallel agents — all write to the shared local SQL server
4. Session ends → `bd dolt push` syncs beads data back to git

## Behavior

- Dolt SQL server starts automatically on each new VM boot via systemd
- All parallel agent sessions connect to the same local server
- Concurrent writes succeed without locking errors
- `.beads/config.yaml` points to the local Dolt SQL server

## Out of scope

- Database initialization (`dolt init`) — `bd bootstrap` owns this
- Data migration from existing embedded Dolt instances
- Remote/shared Dolt server (local to the agent VM only)
- Dolt Cloud or Dolt Hub integration
notes: eudicy/main merged (PR #15). Upstream PR #54 opened: https://github.com/wonderbird/ansible-all-my-things/pull/54 — awaiting wonderbird merge. After upstream merge: run migration (bd backup sync + bd backup restore --force), then close this issue.
close_reason: dolt_sql_server role implemented and merged to eudicy/main. Upstream PR #54 open at wonderbird/ansible-all-my-things.

title: Fix Pinning Lint CI failure on eudicy/main
description:
## Why

Pinning Lint job fails on eudicy/main after merge of fork-safe-docker-ci (commit 07e3ed5).

Run: https://github.com/eudicy/ansible-all-my-things/actions/runs/26385355353

## Two distinct failures

### 1. SARIF upload — CodeQL permission error
`upload-sarif` step fails with:
`Resource not accessible by integration`

zizmor itself exits with code 0 (scan passes). The SARIF upload to CodeQL fails because eudicy fork does not have GitHub Advanced Security / `security-events: write` permission on the GITHUB_TOKEN.

Fix: guard `upload-sarif` step with `if: github.repository == 'wonderbird/ansible-all-my-things'` (or equivalent repo owner check) so SARIF upload only runs on the canonical upstream repo.

### 2. New zizmor audit: `dependabot-cooldown`
zizmor 1.25.2 reports:
`insufficient cooldown in Dependabot updates: missing cooldown configuration`
Severity: Medium | Confidence: High | Rule: `zizmor/dependabot-cooldown`
Docs: https://docs.zizmor.sh/audits/#dependabot-cooldown

Fix: either add cooldown config to `.github/dependabot.yml` or suppress with rationale in `.github/zizmor.yml`.

## Acceptance criteria
- Pinning Lint job passes on eudicy/main
- SARIF upload does not cause job failure on fork
- dependabot-cooldown finding resolved or suppressed with documented rationale
notes:
Fixed in commit 8d9d608 on feature/fork-safe-docker-ci:
1. Replaced deprecated 'args:' input with config:/inputs:/advanced-security: native inputs
2. Added dependabot-cooldown: disable: true to .github/zizmor.yml with rationale
advanced-security: uses event_name != pull_request (capability-based condition)
Waiting for CI on wonderbird PR #48 to confirm green.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 41aaeb1 on eudicy/main. All PR #48 checks pass.

title: Add github/codeql-action to eudicy Actions allow-list
description:
## Why

pinning-lint.yml uses zizmorcore/zizmor-action which internally calls github/codeql-action/upload-sarif to upload SARIF security scan results. The eudicy/ansible-all-my-things repo allow-list does not include github/codeql-action/*, causing the pinning-lint CI job to fail with 'action not allowed' at setup time.

Provenance: found during PR #11 CI run (26368241472), job 77615707865.

## What needs to be done

- Add github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@* to the eudicy/ansible-all-my-things Actions allow-list via GitHub API or Settings
- Update documentation (ADR-002 allow-list section, CONTRIBUTING.md) to include this action in the required allow-list entries for forks

## Acceptance criteria

- PR #11 pinning-lint job passes with no allow-list error
- ADR-002 allow-list setup section lists github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@*
close_reason: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@* added to eudicy allow-list via gh api. ADR-002 current entries updated. pinning-lint now passes on PR #11.

title: Add zizmorcore/zizmor-action to GitHub allow-list and ADR-002
description:
## Why

ADR-002 requires that adding a new action includes both pinning it AND adding it to the GitHub repository allow-list. zizmorcore/zizmor-action was added to pinning-lint.yml (SHA-pinned correctly as Tier A) but was not added to the allow-list in repository settings or to the allow-list section in ADR-002.

## What needs to be done

1. Add zizmorcore/zizmor-action@* to repository Settings → Actions → General → Allow select actions and reusable workflows
2. Add zizmorcore/zizmor-action@* to the Current entries list in the ADR-002 allow-list section (docs/architecture/decisions/002-github-actions-pinning-policy.md)

## Acceptance criteria

- zizmorcore/zizmor-action@* present in GitHub repository allow-list settings
- ADR-002 Current entries list includes zizmorcore/zizmor-action@*
- Both changes consistent with each other
close_reason: zizmorcore/zizmor-action@* already present in allow-list and ADR-002 current entries list.

title: Fix allow-list entries in ADR-002: add trailing commas
description: ADR-002 § Allow-List Configuration lists allow-list entries one per line without commas. This implies newlines work as separators in GitHub's Settings → Actions allow-list textbox, but they do not — GitHub requires comma-separated entries. Anyone copying the code block verbatim will get a broken allow-list. Fix: add trailing comma to each entry line in the code block.
close_reason: Fixed in this session

title: Add prescriptive per-action allow-list MUST to constitution Principle IX rule 4
description: Constitution Principle IX rule 4 currently says 'Each repository instance MUST enable the allow-list per ADR-002 § Allow-List Configuration' (fork setup) and 'See ADR-002 for full criteria' (soft cross-reference). An agent adding a new action may correctly apply the pinning tier rules and stop without following the soft reference to find the allow-list update step. Add a prescriptive sentence: 'When adding a new action to a workflow, agents MUST also add owner/repo@* to the repository allow-list per ADR-002 § Allow-List Configuration.' This closes the gap between a fork-setup MUST and a per-action-addition MUST.
close_reason: Fixed in this session

title: Task 1: Update constitution with Principle IX cross-reference to ADR-002
description:

- PATCH (1.7.0 → 1.7.1) if the cross-reference is purely informational ('see ADR-002 for the current pinning policy')
- MINOR (1.7.0 → 1.8.0) if the cross-reference introduces a normative MUST ('all CI workflows MUST follow ADR-002')

title: Finding 2b: Sweep beads-ID refs from ADR-002 final text

title: Finding 5: Add 'no recurring out-of-pocket cost' as ADR-002 decision driver
description:
ADR-002 Decision Drivers do not currently capture an operational constraint the maintainer wants explicit: this is an open-source personal repository and the maintainer does not want to incur recurring monetary cost to maintain the pinning policy. This is a non-trivial constraint that should weigh in option selection — specifically:

- Option F (buy hardening product, e.g. Harden-Runner) — free tier is acceptable, paid tier is not
- Option G (repository allow-list) — must remain on GitHub Free tier
- Any tooling (pinact, ratchet, zizmor) chosen for the follow-up CI lint — must be free / OSS, no paid SaaS

Fix: add a new decision driver to ADR-002 (provisional name 'D5 — No recurring out-of-pocket cost'), priority somewhere between D2 (auditability) and D3 (patch velocity), and re-test each option against it. Update pros/cons where the driver flips a verdict (most relevantly Option F).

Acceptance:
- New decision driver added with one-sentence rationale referencing the maintainer's open-source posture
- Each option's pros/cons cross-reference D5 where the option has a recurring-cost dimension
- Recommendation paragraph notes whether D5 changed the verdict
- Option F pros/cons explicitly tag paid-tier features as 'D5-violating, defer adoption'
close_reason: Closed

title: Finding 4: Re-evaluate ADR-002 recommendation against mitigations + threat IDs
description:
ADR-002 Mitigations sections currently sit below pros/cons and read as per-option apologetics rather than load-bearing inputs to the Recommendation. After Findings 1, 2, 3 land, re-walk every option through:
  (a) the explicit threat IDs added in Finding 3, and
  (b) each option's mitigations

and test whether the Recommendation still stands as bare Option A.

Specific cases worth re-evaluating:
- Option B + auto-merge for actions/* SHA bumps + pinact tooling: D3 (patch velocity) gap closes substantially. Residual delta = trust in auto-merge. If acceptable, B becomes competitive (simpler rule, uniform SHA, no Tier B identity-trust dependency).
- Option C + Option F (Harden-Runner): runtime egress monitoring catches what tag-retargeting opens up. Worse on auditability (D2). Possibly acceptable for this repo's blast radius.
- Option A + Option G (allow-list): ADR already names this combo but does not commit to it as the recommendation. If recommended, recommendation should be 'A+G', not bare A.

Method: after Findings 1, 2, 3 land, do the option-vs-threat-vs-mitigation matrix walk. Update Recommendation accordingly. If no shift, document why explicitly.

Meta-fix: the Mitigations sections should be positioned in the ADR such that the reader cannot read pros/cons without already knowing the mitigations. (Note: skill-level fix tracked separately as Finding 6.)

Acceptance:
- Recommendation paragraph cites threat IDs and mitigations explicitly
- Each rejected option includes a one-sentence 'rejected even with mitigations because ...' justification
- If Recommendation shifts (e.g. to A+G or B+auto-merge), Sync Impact and follow-up tasks updated accordingly
close_reason: Closed

title: Finding 3: Add explicit threat model section to ADR-002
description:
ADR-002 currently jumps from mixed-pinning observation to mechanism (Tier A/B) without enumerating the threats the policy is supposed to address. A decision-maker has to reverse-engineer the threat model from the pros/cons.

Fix: insert an explicit Threat Model section between Context and Decision Drivers. Enumerate threats and, for each, note in scope vs out of scope and whether the recommended option addresses it.

Candidate threats to discuss:
1. Pipeline credential leak — GHCR push token + OIDC ID token exposed to malicious action code running in the push job. (Highest blast radius.)
2. Malicious artefact published — image carrying backdoor under our signed identity; cosign signature still valid because we sign whatever we built. Downstream trust chain weaponized.
3. CI secret exfil — Ansible Vault password, SSH keys, cloud creds leaked via secrets.* env exposure to action code. (Currently low-surface in this repo's CI but framing should not assume that.)
4. Supply chain to maintainer dev box — compromised action writes malicious content into .devcontainer/ that gets pulled to local laptops. Indirect attack path.
5. Phishing / social-engineered PRs — adversary opens PR adding 'uses: evil/foo@v1' betting on reviewer fatigue. Policy + lint reduces blast radius.
6. Bot-net abuse of forks — fork CI as crypto miner / proxy. Likely out of scope; name and dismiss.

After enumeration: map each Considered Option to which threats it materially reduces. Reader should be able to see at a glance why Option A is recommended for the threat profile of this repo.

Acceptance:
- New Threat Model section exists between Context and Decision Drivers
- Each threat states: in/out of scope, owning option, severity for this repo
- Pros/cons of each option cross-reference threat IDs
- Recommendation paragraph cites threat IDs explicitly

Note: this finding pairs with Finding 4 (re-evaluate mitigations against threats). Once threats are explicit, the right mitigations become testable. Keep this finding scoped to the threat-model section; Finding 4 handles the solution re-evaluation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Finding 2: Forbid beads-ID refs in durable artefacts; add constitution principle + sweep existing refs
description:
Beads issue IDs are ephemeral — closed issues get purged with no archive of descriptions. Any code/doc/ADR/constitution reference to a beads ID becomes a dead string the moment the issue closes, leaving the reader with no way to recover what the reference meant.

Current ADR-002 itself contains such refs: urf, v2u, i3p, 86r appear inline in the Confirmation, Enforcement, More Information, and Mitigation sections.

Substance MUST be inlined. Beads refs remain allowed in PR descriptions and commit message bodies (which become immutable git history once committed) and in .omc/ working files.

title: Replace curl|sudo mv of container-structure-test /latest/ binary in CI
description:
Code-review finding from ADR-002 reviews. docker-publish.yml test job (lines ~107-110) downloads container-structure-test from a floating /latest/ download URL and sudo-mv's it into /usr/local/bin/ on every run, with no checksum or signature verification. The test job is needs: of the push job which holds packages:write + id-token:write — so a compromised /latest/ binary executes inside the credential path of the publish pipeline.

This is independent of the pinning policy (ADR-002). It is a separate supply-chain finding that ADR-002 explicitly lists as out of scope (and references this issue).

Acceptance:
- container-structure-test installed via pinned release tag (not /latest/)
- Either: SHA256 checksum verified against an upstream-published value, OR cosign verify of the released artefact if upstream signs releases
- No floating-version downloads in any job that is a needs: ancestor of the push job
- Document chosen approach in a short note inside docker-publish.yml header

Out of scope: switching tool, replacing with a GitHub Action wrapper (separate eval).
close_reason: Closed

title: Add CI lint job to enforce GitHub Actions pinning policy
description:
Enforce the two-tier pinning policy defined in ADR 002 (follow-up from finding urf). Add a workflow that fails the build when Tier A actions (third-party: docker/*, sigstore/*, anything with packages:write / id-token:write / signing / registry push) are pinned by tag instead of 40-char SHA. Tier B (first-party actions/* utilities) may keep floating major tags.

Tool evaluation REQUIRED before selection — assess upstream activity and health for each candidate via GitHub MCP or gh CLI (last release, commit cadence, open-issue trend, maintainer count, archived flag, CVE history):

- zizmor (Rust, built-in unpinned-uses audit)
- pinact (auto-pins + --check mode)
- ratchet (Go, ratchet check)

Record findings + chosen tool + rejection rationale in design field. Fallback: hand-rolled regex grep if all candidates fail health check.

Acceptance:
- Tool selected with documented health-check evidence
- Lint runs in CI on push + PR
- Tier A tag-pin in any workflow fails the job
- Tier B tag-pin passes
- Allow-list mechanism for documented exceptions (comment-based or config)
- README / ADR 002 cross-link to the lint job

Out of scope: rewriting existing pins (already conformant), changing the policy itself.
design: Tool health check (2026-05-21): zizmor (woodruffw/zizmor) v1.25.2 — 5182 stars, pushed 2026-05-20, not archived, most active project; pinact (suzuki-shunsuke/pinact) v3.10.1 — 1020 stars, pushed 2026-05-21, not archived, active but lacks native Tier A/B policy layer; ratchet (sethvargo/ratchet) v0.11.4 — 935 stars, last release 2025-06-27 (~11 months), slower cadence. Selected: zizmor. Rejection rationale — pinact: check mode flags all non-SHA pins uniformly, no built-in ref-pin policy for actions/* Tier B; ratchet: 11-month release gap indicates slower maintenance. zizmor provides native unpinned-uses.config.policies supporting hash-pin vs ref-pin per pattern, directly encoding the two-tier policy without wrappers. Config: .github/zizmor.yml. Workflow: .github/workflows/pinning-lint.yml. Verified: conformant workflows exit 0; docker/login-action@v3 (Tier A tag-pin) exits 14 with high-severity unpinned-uses error; actions/checkout@v6 (Tier B) exits 0.
close_reason: Closed

title: Finding 2: Establish GitHub Actions pinning policy (SHA vs version tags)
description: Audit current pinning strategy in .github/workflows/docker-publish.yml. Currently mixed: SHA pins on docker/*, sigstore/* (artifact/credential actions) vs version tags on checkout, upload/download-artifact (utility actions). Create explicit policy document specifying which action categories warrant SHA pinning vs which can use version tags with Dependabot auto-patching. Rationale: SHAs prevent tag retagging attacks (immutable), version tags enable faster security patch velocity via Dependabot. Trade-off: readability vs maintainability vs attack surface. Aligns with Principle IX (CI/CD Pipeline Security) — explicit, auditable artifact provenance chain.
close_reason: ADR-002 accepted. Pinning policy established as A+G (two-tier policy + GitHub allow-list). This finding is resolved.

title: Refine docker-publish.yml: build → test → push order
description:
## Why

After ngu implements fork-safe artifact passing, the non-PR path (push to main/tags) still pushes to GHCR before running tests. This means a broken image can land in the registry.

## What needs to be done

Reorganize docker-publish.yml so the pipeline enforces build → test → push on ALL paths:

1. Build job: build image and export as tarball artifact (already done for PR path via ngu)
2. Test job: download artifact, load image, run container-structure-test — same job for both PR and non-PR paths
3. Push job: only runs after test job succeeds AND event is not a pull_request; pulls tarball artifact, loads image, pushes to GHCR, signs with cosign

This ensures GHCR only ever receives images that have passed structure tests.

## Acceptance criteria

- On PR: build → test → no push (same as ngu outcome)
- On push to main/tag: build → test → push (push only if test passes)
- No image ever reaches GHCR without a passing test job
- Cosign signing still runs after push
close_reason: build→test→push enforced on all paths. PR #6 merged to eudicy/ansible-all-my-things main. Run 25940172292 confirms: build×2, test×2, push×2 all pass. latest+latest-amd64 pushed and cosign-signed (sha256:578f41ef...). latest-arm64 pushed by arm64 leg.

title: Reduce Docker image size with multi-stage build
description:
## Why

Current .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses a single stage — build tools (pip,
pipx, git, python3-venv, gcc etc.) end up in the runtime image, inflating
size unnecessarily. Large images slow CI pull times and increase storage cost.

## Solution

Adopt multi-stage build pattern from BadWinniePooh/ansible-collection
(.docker/Dockerfile, sha b51cf5f):

- **builder stage**: full toolchain — python3, pip, pipx, git, openssh-client.
  Installs ansible-core via pipx, runs pip install -r requirements.txt,
  runs ansible-galaxy collection install -r requirements.yml.
- **runtime stage**: only python3 + openssh-client apt packages.
  Copy /root/.local (pipx venv + wrappers) and /root/.ansible (collections)
  from builder. No compilers, no pip, no pipx binary, no git in final image.

## Acceptance criteria

- .devcontainer/Dockerfile uses FROM ... AS builder + second FROM for runtime
- Final image contains ansible-playbook, ansible-galaxy executables
- Final image does NOT contain pip, pipx, git binaries
- container-structure-test suite passes on the new image
- Image size reduced vs. current single-stage build (document before/after in commit)
notes:
ubuntu:26.04 adopted for both stages. Key findings:
1. gpg-agent must be explicitly installed in ubuntu builder (not auto-dep of gpg unlike Debian)
2. AWS CLI PyInstaller binary uses $ORIGIN RPATH — when COPY breaks the symlink chain (from /usr/local/bin/aws into dist/), the bootloader looks for libpython3.14.so.1.0 in /usr/local/bin/ and fails. Fix: symlink /usr/local/bin/aws -> /usr/local/aws-cli/v2/current/bin/aws so bootloader resolves the real path inside dist/ where the bundled library lives.
3. locales-all dropped (was 300MB uncompressed); ubuntu base has locales package sufficient for ansible.
4. Final image: 686 MB. Tests: 6/6 pass.
5. container-structure-test needs Podman socket active: systemctl --user start podman.socket
close_reason: ubuntu:26.04 base adopted; all 6 container-structure-tests pass; image 686 MB

title: Fork-safe Docker CI: fix docker-publish.yml for fork compatibility
description:
## Why

docker-publish.yml hardcodes 'wonderbird/ansible-toolchain' as image name. On forks:
- Push steps fail (read-only GITHUB_TOKEN)
- Test jobs validate wrong image (upstream :latest, not the built one)
- Both architectures untested on PRs

Breaks CI for all contributors working on forks.

## What needs to be done

Implement the design from docs/features/fork-safe-docker-ci/:

1. Replace hardcoded image name with `${{ github.repository_owner }}/ansible-toolchain`
2. Split build into per-arch matrix: ubuntu-24.04 (amd64) + ubuntu-24.04-arm (arm64)
3. PR path: build → docker save tarball → upload artifact → test job downloads + loads
4. Non-PR path: push to ghcr.io → test job pulls from registry
5. Scope GHA cache keys per arch
6. Guard cosign/push steps with `github.event_name != 'pull_request'`

## Acceptance criteria

- PR from any fork: both archs build and test with no registry push
- Push to main: images published to fork owner's ghcr.io namespace
- No hardcoded 'wonderbird' in workflow file
notes: Feature branch: feature/fork-safe-docker-ci

Two bugs: (1) line 27 reads 'ansible-playbook configure-profile.yml -i ...' missing the 'playbooks/' prefix present on every other invocation in the file (create-vm.yml, destroy-vm.yml) — command fails with [ERROR]: the playbook: configure-profile.yml could not be found. title: Validate desktop-profile feature on real tart VM, AWS, and docker rejection (tasks T011a/T014/T016/T018/T021)
description: Implementation of specs/014-desktop-profile is complete and syntax-checked, but Constitution Principle III requires real-target validation before this can be considered done, and the coding-agent sandbox has neither tart (macOS-only) nor docker nor AWS credentials available. Manual steps from specs/014-desktop-profile/quickstart.md and tasks.md still needed: T011a (diff basic-profile role/file behavior before/after the hosts:basic re-scope), T014 (create-vm.yml -e provider=tart -e profile=desktop then configure-profile.yml --limit <name>; confirm XFCE/XRDP, keyring, desktop apps, full legacy role list), T016 (provider=docker -e profile=desktop fails immediately; tart/hcloud/aws -e profile=desktop still succeed), T018 (AWS: profile=desktop opens 3389 on ansible-sg; a later profile=basic create does not remove it), T021 (full quickstart.md pass: tart desktop, docker+desktop rejection, AWS RDP scoping).
close_reason: All 5 validation tasks passed on real infra: T011a (basic-profile regression, host romulus), T014 (tart desktop MVP end-to-end, host romulus), T016 (docker+desktop rejection, immediate fail naming both values), T018 (AWS RDP scoping, ansible-sg gained 3389 for desktop, unchanged for basic), T021 (quickstart.md commands all exercised live with passing results). Two findings discovered and fixed along the way: ansible-all-my-things-o9ou (exa MCP password/API key leaked in plaintext, fixed with no_log: true, secrets rotated) and ansible-all-my-things-znlt (quickstart.md wrong playbook path + missing ARM64 --skip-tags note, fixed). Test VMs/instances destroyed and cleanup verified.

title: Constitution Governance scopes Complexity Tracking to Principle IV only, but practice uses it generically
description: constitution.md:398 says 'Complexity exceptions (violations of Principle IV) MUST be documented in the Complexity Tracking table of the relevant plan.md before implementation begins.' This literally scopes the requirement to Principle IV (Simplicity/YAGNI) only. In practice, plan.md files (this feature's specs/014-desktop-profile/plan.md, and prior precedent specs/013-aws-vm-provider/plan.md) use the same Complexity Tracking table generically for any principle exception -- here a Principle II (Role-First Organisation) exception for verbatim-importing legacy desktop playbooks. Found during /speckit-analyze on specs/014-desktop-profile. Not a defect in the current feature (over-complying isn't a violation), but the constitution's wording should be generalized to 'violations of any Core Principle' to match actual usage, via a separate constitution amendment (not part of this feature).
close_reason: Amended constitution 1.18.0->1.18.1: generalized Complexity Tracking sentence from Principle IV-only to any Core Principle, matching existing practice

title: Gate Phase 6 legacy deletion on a proven restore.yml round-trip
description: roadmap.md defers porting restore.yml to immediately before Phase 6 deletes the legacy stack -- the new restore path is least proven exactly when the only fallback disappears. Add an explicit backup-destroy-create-restore round-trip as a stated Phase 6 entry gate. See docs/feature-requests/feat.create-destroy-vm/FINDINGS.md #4.

title: Make AWS desktop-profile RDP (3389) rule conditional, not additive
description: tasks/create/aws.yml:93-99 uses one shared ansible-sg group with purge_rules:false for every AWS VM. Adding 3389 additively for the desktop case would open RDP on basic-profile AWS VMs too. Needs to be conditioned on profile==desktop or use a separate security group. See docs/feature-requests/feat.create-destroy-vm/FINDINGS.md #3.
close_reason: Implemented: tasks/create/aws.yml's ansible-sg rules list is now built via Jinja, appending the TCP 3389 rule only when profile=='desktop'; profile=basic creates only the TCP 22 rule.

title: Define profile-to-inventory-group binding for desktop profile role play
description: configure-profile-roles.yml:3 and legacy configure-linux-roles.yml:3 both target hosts: linux, no profile scoping. Verbatim-importing the legacy role list under profile=desktop has no mechanism to keep it off basic-profile hosts (concrete symptom: tmux_install_iconic_font play var vs role default). specs/010 research recorded profiles must not share one mutable role list. See docs/feature-requests/feat.create-destroy-vm/FINDINGS.md #2.
close_reason: Implemented: configure-profile-roles.yml re-scoped to hosts:basic; new import_playbook of root configure-linux-roles.yml gated by when: "'desktop' in group_names" applies the legacy role list only to desktop-group hosts.

title: Log Phase 5a Principle II exception in a plan.md Complexity Tracking table
description: Architect review found the accepted Principle II exception for Phase 5a (verbatim-import legacy desktop stack) exists only as prose in roadmap.md. Governance (constitution.md:397-399) requires it logged in a plan.md Complexity Tracking table before implementation begins. No plan.md exists for the desktop-profile phase. See docs/feature-requests/feat.create-destroy-vm/FINDINGS.md #1.
close_reason: specs/014-desktop-profile/plan.md Complexity Tracking table (committed in 630abf9) already logs the Principle II exception for the verbatim-import of setup-desktop.yml/setup-keyring.yml/setup-desktop-apps.yml/configure-linux-roles.yml's role list.

title: Add tmux role to basic profile (configure-profile-roles.yml)
description: Legacy configure-linux-roles.yml gives every machine tmux unconditionally. New configure-profile-roles.yml (basic profile, 5 roles: podman/ruby/python/dolt_sql_server/claude_code) dropped tmux — confirmed absent on main HEAD, not a stale-branch issue. PR #34 (jqmx-tmux-console-only-font) only renamed tmux_install_nerd_font->tmux_install_iconic_font inside the tmux role itself; never added tmux back to the basic profile's role list. Add the tmux role to configure-profile-roles.yml's basic-profile play.

title: molecule default scenario for tmux: container died mid-converge (exec session error)
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. cd roles/tmux
2. molecule test --scenario-name default

## Observed
create phase succeeds, container created. converge phase fails immediately at
"Gathering Facts" with:
  Error deleting remote temporary files (rc: 255, stderr: Error: can only
  create exec sessions on running containers: container state improper)
Container appears to have died/stopped between create and converge.

## Expected
Container stays running through converge; this is suspected to be a transient
podman race (possibly related to a parallel agent also using podman/molecule
on the same host - container/image names are shared at the podman-engine
level even across separate git worktrees), not caused by the tmux_install_nerd_font
change. Hypothesis under test: re-run is sufficient (transient), no code fix needed.
acceptance_criteria: molecule test --scenario-name default passes cleanly for roles/tmux
close_reason: Root cause confirmed via journalctl: not a tmux-role defect. A concurrent agent session running molecule for the claude_code role did 'podman rm --force instance' + recreated container 'instance' for its own scenario at the exact moment this scenario's container died. All molecule scenarios in this project hardcode platform name 'instance', which collides across concurrent sessions on one podman host (git worktrees do not isolate the podman container namespace). User paused the concurrent session; retrying now that it's clear.

title: claude_code molecule Dockerfile bakes in git/pipx, masking role's own missing prereqs
description: Discovered while verifying ansible-all-my-things-g9jb (missing git prereq fix). roles/claude_code/molecule/default/Dockerfile installs python3 sudo git ca-certificates pipx. Per the documented image policy in playbooks/files/docker/Dockerfile (managed-node surrogate must NOT bake in tool prereqs like git/curl — each role installs its own, so a role that forgets a prereq fails the test instead of being masked), the molecule container for claude_code should follow the same discipline. Baking in git masked the exact bug just fixed in g9jb (install-deps.yml didn't install git) — molecule test passed throughout even though the role would fail on a real fresh host. Same masking applies to pipx: install-addons.yml:253 runs 'pipx install ...' but no task in the role ever installs pipx itself; the molecule Dockerfile papers over this by preinstalling it. Fix: remove git and pipx from the Dockerfile (keep only python3/sudo/ca-certificates as connection substrate), add a pipx apt-install task to install-deps.yml (matching the git/jq pattern), and rerun molecule test to confirm the role is now fully self-contained.
close_reason: Fixed: removed git/pipx from molecule Dockerfile, added pipx apt task, reordered prepare.yml's curl install before nvm install. Molecule test passes full lifecycle (41 ok, 0 failed), role now fully self-contained.

title: tmux role fails on console-only Linux (Hack Nerd font requires desktop env)
description:
## Steps to Reproduce
1. Configure a console-only Linux VM via configure-profile.yml.
2. Add/run the tmux role against that VM (no desktop environment present).
3. Observe installation failure.

## Observed
Installation fails because Hack Nerd Font cannot be installed — it requires a desktop
environment. Root cause: roles/tmux/tasks/install-font.yml unconditionally loops over
desktop_user_names and installs the font, with no guard for console-only targets.

## Expected
tmux role succeeds on a console-only target; font step is skipped there.
acceptance_criteria:
- tmux role MUST succeed on a console-only Linux environment (no desktop env present).
- Hack Nerd Font MUST still be installed when a desktop Linux environment is present.
- Default acceptance criteria from the constitution and AGENTS.md apply (idempotency,
  Molecule test coverage, version-update wiring if applicable, etc.).
close_reason: Fixed: added tmux_install_nerd_font (default false) gating the font import in roles/tmux/tasks/main.yml; configure-linux-roles.yml opts in for the real desktop play. Verified via two Molecule scenarios (default: font absent/console-safe, with-font: font present), both green incl. idempotence. Committed 071c20a on branch jqmx-tmux-console-only-font.

title: claude_code role: install-addons fails with 'Failed to find required executable git'
description: Running configure-profile.yml + configure-profile-roles.yml against a fresh docker host fails at roles/claude_code/tasks/install-addons.yml:7 ('Clone oh-my-claudecode source repository', ansible.builtin.git module): 'Failed to find required executable "git" in paths: ...'. Root cause: roles/claude_code/tasks/install-deps.yml installs jq and rtk as role prerequisites but never installs git, even though install-addons.yml uses the ansible.builtin.git module 4 times (oh-my-claudecode, beads, ai-agent-workspace clones, plus an implicit git dependency for pipx/spec-kit install via git+https URL). Fix: add a git apt-install task to install-deps.yml, matching the existing jq task pattern, so the role is self-contained per Principle II (Role-First Organisation).
close_reason: Verified on tatooine: configure-profile.yml + configure-profile-roles.yml run clean, second run idempotent (changed=0). git + pipx prereqs now self-installed by claude_code role.

title: setup-nodejs.yml: NVM install fails on fresh VM — 'You need git, curl, or wget to install nvm'
description: On a fresh docker-based VM, running 'ansible-playbook playbooks/configure-profile.yml -l tatooine' fails at the 'Install NVM' task in playbooks/setup-nodejs.yml:17. The nvm install.sh script (run via become_user under shell) reports: stderr 'You need git, curl, or wget to install nvm'. Likely cause: fresh VM lacks curl/wget/git, or PATH is stripped under become_user non-interactive shell so an installed binary isn't found. Need to confirm which on a reproducer VM, then either ensure curl/git is a precondition package on linux hosts before setup-nodejs.yml runs, or pass an explicit PATH/use a module that doesn't depend on shell PATH resolution.
notes: Root cause confirmed: playbooks/configure-profile.yml runs setup-nodejs.yml (line 4) before any apt package install. Fresh VM has no curl/git/wget. Fix: added 'Install curl' apt task to playbooks/setup-nodejs.yml tasks, before the NVM download/install tasks, mirroring roles/java/tasks/main.yml prereq pattern. Awaiting user test run on tatooine VM before close.
close_reason: Fix verified on tatooine: first run succeeded, second run idempotent (changed=0, failed=0).

title: molecule tests cannot run on macOS — Podman driver hardcoded in all scenarios
description:
All 6 testable roles (claude_code, dolt_sql_server, java, python, ruby, tmux) hardcode `driver: name: podman` in their `molecule/default/molecule.yml`. The `podman` role scenario is intentionally Podman-only and out of scope.

On macOS, Podman is not available by default. There is no runtime override that switches the driver plugin — `MOLECULE_DRIVER_NAME` env var changes the name string but molecule still loads Podman's create/destroy playbooks. Contributors on macOS cannot run any molecule tests locally.

## Acceptance criteria

- `molecule test` can be run locally on a macOS host without installing Podman for all 6 affected roles

title: Convert prepare.yml NVM install from curl|bash to get_url + shell
description:
roles/claude_code/molecule/default/prepare.yml:23 uses curl -fsSL ... | bash to install NVM for testuser. The molecule Dockerfile (roles/claude_code/molecule/default/Dockerfile) ships curl as an ambient tool, making molecule tests weaker than the real cloud target (same false-negative risk that ko1q fixed in the provider image).

Fix requires two changes:
1. prepare.yml: convert NVM install from curl|bash to ansible.builtin.get_url + shell, using the same pattern as playbooks/setup-nodejs.yml (get_url to /tmp/nvm-install.sh, then shell: cmd: /tmp/nvm-install.sh with creates: guard).
2. roles/claude_code/molecule/default/Dockerfile: remove curl from apt install list.

Without both changes, the prepare.yml conversion is cosmetic — curl remains ambient so tests pass regardless. Both changes together make molecule tests as strict as the real target.
close_reason: Converted NVM install from curl|bash to get_url+shell (v0.40.3), removed curl from Dockerfile, explicitly apt-install curl before nvm install --lts. Molecule tests pass.

title: Provider Docker image: remove ambient curl, keep ca-certificates as substrate, make roles self-install prereqs
description:
Docker provider image (playbooks/files/docker/Dockerfile) is a local stand-in for AWS/Hetzner VM (managed node), NOT the control node (.devcontainer). curl was added to image as band-aid for two roles assuming ambient curl. This contradicts established pattern: roles self-install own apt prereqs (java: roles/java/tasks/main.yml installs zip/unzip/curl; setup-homebrew.yml installs curl).

CONCLUSION: curl in provider image hides broken roles + makes local Docker test weaker than worst-case cloud target (false negative). ca-certificates is different: TLS trust substrate that ~10 get_url roles depend on; per-role install = DRY violation; every cloud Ubuntu ships it. ca-certificates stays. None of devcontainer's other tools (git, gpg, unzip, wget, pipx, jq, vim) belong in target image - each control-node or role-specific.

RECOMMENDATION (image final set: systemd, systemd-sysv, dbus, openssh-server, sudo, python3, ca-certificates):
1. Convert curl|bash sites to ansible.builtin.get_url + run script (native, idempotent, fails loud - also fixes the original nvm bug chain, Principles I/VII/XII):
   - playbooks/setup-nodejs.yml:12 (nvm install)
   - roles/claude_code/tasks/install-addons.yml:24,34 (beads, beads_viewer)
2. Remove curl from playbooks/files/docker/Dockerfile; keep ca-certificates.
3. Leave java/homebrew as-is (genuine curl-binary callers, already self-install).
4. Add NO new tools to image.
5. Add rationale summary comment on top of the Dockerfile so the curl-out/ca-certs-in decision is recorded for future.
design: Image = managed-node surrogate, must mirror worst-case cloud target and stay strict so missing-prereq bugs fail locally. Roles own their tool prereqs. ca-certificates = shared TLS substrate, kept central to avoid DRY violation across get_url roles.
acceptance_criteria: curl removed from playbooks/files/docker/Dockerfile; ca-certificates retained; rationale comment present at top of Dockerfile; curl|bash converted to get_url in setup-nodejs.yml and claude_code/install-addons.yml; no role depends on ambient curl binary except java/homebrew which self-install it
close_reason: curl removed from Dockerfile; ca-certificates retained; rationale comment added; curl|bash converted to get_url+shell in setup-nodejs.yml and install-addons.yml; README updated

title: docker create: delegate_to 127.0.0.1 ignores SSH conn vars (implicit localhost=local)
description:
3 tasks in playbooks/tasks/create/docker.yml use delegate_to: 127.0.0.1 with SSH conn vars (ansible_port, ansible_user, ansible_ssh_pass, ansible_ssh_common_args) but no ansible_connection: ssh. Ansible's implicit-localhost rule forces ansible_connection: local for 127.0.0.1/localhost targets not in inventory, so these vars are silently ignored - module/probe runs on the controller, not in the container.

Observed: 'Install SSH public key for root on the container' failed with 'Permission denied on file or path for authorized keys file: /var/root/.ssh/authorized_keys' - /var/root is the macOS root home, proving the task ran on the controller (macOS), not the Linux container.

Also affects: 'Wait for SSH on container' (false-pass, local connection trivially succeeds) and 'Probe key-only SSH login as root' (would also false-pass).

Tart (playbooks/tasks/create/tart.yml) unaffected: delegate_to is a real VM IP, not 127.0.0.1, so implicit-localhost rule doesn't trigger.

Fix: add ansible_connection: ssh to vars: of all 3 delegate_to 127.0.0.1 tasks in docker.yml.

Found during e2e test of branch 076-uniform-ssh-key-auth-local-providers (gate ansible-all-my-things-nk80, blocks xby9).
acceptance_criteria: docker create-vm.yml run succeeds end to end: container created, SSH pubkey installed for root inside container, key-only probe passes against the container (not controller), inventory written without ansible_ssh_pass. configure-profile.yml first and second run against the docker host both succeed (Gathering Facts ok, second run not UNREACHABLE).
notes: Second symptom of same root cause surfaced after fixing ansible_connection: implicit-localhost also pins ansible_python_interpreter to the controller's (macOS) python path, invalid inside the container, so the ping module never succeeds and wait_for_connection times out ('timed out waiting for ping module test'). Manual sshpass test confirmed SSH/auth/python3 all work fine inside the container - confirming the issue is purely the inherited ansible_python_interpreter var. Fix: add ansible_python_interpreter: auto_silent alongside ansible_connection: ssh in all 3 delegate_to 127.0.0.1 tasks.
close_reason: Both root causes fixed (ansible_connection: ssh + ansible_python_interpreter: auto_silent) and verified e2e: docker create-vm + configure-profile 1st/2nd run all pass.

title: E2E findings: docker provider create (076-uniform-ssh-key-auth-local-providers)
description: Gate for findings discovered during manual e2e test of branch 076-uniform-ssh-key-auth-local-providers, connected to active WIP xby9 (in_progress).
close_reason: All findings from e2e test resolved (xoe7).

title: Reconcile 009/011 spec narrative with key-based local-provider auth
description:
## Context

ADR-004 + the xby9 fix switched the docker and tart create flows from
password-based inventory auth (ansible_ssh_pass) to key-based auth
(my_ssh_public_key installed at create, inventory connects by key).

The interface tables in specs/009-create-destroy-vm/data-model.md and
specs/011-docker-vm-provider/data-model.md were updated in that change. But
narrative prose still describes the old password mechanism and now drifts from
the codebase (Constitution governance: specs must stay consistent with the
interfaces they describe).

## Remaining drift to reconcile

- specs/011-docker-vm-provider/spec.md (FR-005 inventory entry mentions
  ansible_ssh_pass)
- specs/011-docker-vm-provider/research.md (password-auth rationale)
- specs/011-docker-vm-provider/tasks.md (inventory shape note)
- specs/009-create-destroy-vm/spec.md and research.md (ansible_ssh_pass: admin)
- specs/010-configure-basic-profile/* references to ansible_ssh_pass: admin
- specs/012 / 013 mentions are already key-based (no change expected) — verify.

## Scope

Documentation reconciliation only — no code change. Update narrative to point at
key-based auth and ADR-004; keep the historical decision record intact where it
is explicitly historical.

## Provenance

Discovered while implementing the xby9 fix (uniform key-based auth). Blocks
xby9 closure per Constitution Principle VIII.
close_reason: Re-determined and absorbed into xby9 (commit ef870dd). The required updates were only the 3 interface/contract statements (009/011 spec FR-005, 010 contract precondition) + the 2 data-model tables already done. research.md/tasks.md deliberately NOT rewritten — point-in-time records superseded by ADR-004, not by editing history (Constitution: specs stay consistent for interfaces; git records evolution). 012/013 already key-based. Original 3ox6 scope was too broad and mis-framed mandatory interface fixes as a deferred follow-up.

title: configure-profile.yml not idempotent across runs: second run connects as admin_user_on_fresh_system and fails publickey
description:
## Symptom

Running playbooks/configure-profile.yml a SECOND time against an
already-configured host fails at Gathering Facts — UNREACHABLE:

```
TASK [Gathering Facts]
[ERROR]: Task failed: Failed to connect to the host via ssh: Warning:
Permanently added '[127.0.0.1]:61571' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
root@127.0.0.1: Permission denied (publickey).
fatal: [tatooine]: UNREACHABLE! => {"changed": false, "msg": "...root@127.0.0.1: Permission denied (publickey).", "unreachable": true}
```

## Root cause

playbooks/setup-users.yml line 7 hardcodes the connection user to the
bootstrap account:

    vars:
      ansible_user: "{{ admin_user_on_fresh_system }}"

admin_user_on_fresh_system is the FRESH-system bootstrap login (root on
hcloud, ubuntu on aws). On the first run the play connects as that account,
creates my_ansible_user, installs its SSH key, grants passwordless sudo, and
disables SSH password auth.

On a SECOND run the host is no longer fresh: the intended steady-state login
is my_ansible_user, not the bootstrap account. But the play still forces
ansible_user = admin_user_on_fresh_system (root). Root key-based SSH no longer
succeeds on the configured host, so the connection is refused with
'Permission denied (publickey)' before any task runs.

The play is idempotent in its tasks (Principle I) but the CONNECTION identity
is not run-invariant: it assumes a fresh system every time. Re-running a
configure on an existing host is a normal operation and must work.

## Expected

configure-profile.yml (specifically setup-users.yml) connects as the correct
user regardless of whether the host is fresh or already configured:
- fresh host -> connect as admin_user_on_fresh_system (bootstrap)
- already-configured host -> connect as my_ansible_user (steady state)

so a second run is reachable and idempotent.

## Notes

- Observed host: tatooine, 127.0.0.1:61571 (local port-forwarded VM).
- Related but distinct from the just-fixed group_vars bug (undefined
  admin_user_on_fresh_system on generated-inventory hosts) — that was a
  missing var; this is the var being WRONG on a re-run.
- Fix approach is open (e.g. detect reachable user, or separate bootstrap
  play from steady-state configure). Decide during implementation.

## Constraints

- Constitution Principle I (Idempotency), Principle XII (Fail Loud).
- Commit via commit skill; push origin only (fork).
close_reason: Verified e2e on macOS for both local providers: tart (vulcan) and docker (tatooine). create-vm installs my_ssh_public_key + drops ansible_ssh_pass; configure-profile.yml 1st and 2nd run both succeed (Gathering Facts ok, 2nd run idempotent ok=12 changed=0). Required 2 follow-up fixes for docker (xoe7, now closed).

title: hcloud/aws generated-inventory hosts miss admin_user_on_fresh_system in group_vars
description:
## Symptom

configure-profile.yml (and old configure-linux.yml) fail on cloud-provider
hosts created via the generated-inventory flow:

  [ERROR] Task failed: Error processing keyword 'remote_user':
  'admin_user_on_fresh_system' is undefined
  Origin: playbooks/setup-users.yml:7

playbooks/setup-users.yml line 7 sets:
  ansible_user: "{{ admin_user_on_fresh_system }}"

## Root cause

Repo has TWO inventory systems for cloud:

1. Dynamic (old): inventories/hcloud.yml, inventories/aws_ec2.yml -> plugins
   query cloud API, group hosts into hcloud_linux / aws_ec2_linux.
2. Generated (new, create-destroy-vm epic 009/011/012/013):
   playbooks/tasks/create/{hcloud,aws}.yml write
   inventories/{hcloud,aws}_autogenerated.yml, grouping hosts into
   all / linux / hcloud | aws.

admin_user_on_fresh_system is defined in group_vars/hcloud_linux/vars.yml
(root) and group_vars/aws_ec2_linux/vars.yml (ubuntu). Those groups match
the DYNAMIC inventory. Generated-inventory hosts land in groups hcloud / aws,
which have NO admin_user_on_fresh_system -> undefined at configure time.

Already fixed for docker provider: inventories/group_vars/docker/vars.yml
(admin_user_on_fresh_system: "root"), commit 041fc2a. This issue does the
same for hcloud + aws, using approach B (see below).

## Decision: approach B (DRY for hcloud)

Verified group membership:
- DYNAMIC hcloud (inventories/hcloud.yml) has `group: hcloud` -> old hosts
  ARE in group `hcloud`. So group `hcloud` is the common denominator of old
  (dynamic) AND new (generated) hcloud hosts. Define admin_user_on_fresh_system
  ONCE in group_vars/hcloud/ -> serves both, no duplication.
- DYNAMIC aws (inventories/aws_ec2.yml) has NO `group: aws` (keyed_groups
  produce linux + aws_ec2_linux only). So old aws hosts are NEVER in group
  `aws`. New group_vars/aws/ only affects generated hosts. No shared group ->
  cannot dedup; a separate file is required regardless.

## Required changes

### 1. hcloud — MOVE the var (do not duplicate)

Edit inventories/group_vars/hcloud_linux/vars.yml: REMOVE the line
  admin_user_on_fresh_system: "root"
(keep hcloud_default_type: "cx33" and all comments).

Edit inventories/group_vars/hcloud/vars.yml: ADD
  admin_user_on_fresh_system: "root"
(file already exists, currently defines hcloud_default_location only).

Rationale: group `hcloud` covers old dynamic + new generated hosts; single
home. group_vars/hcloud_linux/ keeps only hcloud_default_type.

### 2. aws — CREATE new file

Create inventories/group_vars/aws/vars.yml:
  ---
  # AWS (Linux) generated-inventory group override
  admin_user_on_fresh_system: "ubuntu"

Leave group_vars/aws_ec2_linux/vars.yml unchanged (still read at create time
via include_vars in playbooks/tasks/create/aws.yml for AMI/instance-type, and
still serves old dynamic aws hosts which use group aws_ec2_linux).

DO NOT remove admin_user_on_fresh_system from aws_ec2_linux: old dynamic aws
hosts are in aws_ec2_linux (not aws), so they still need it there. (Unlike
hcloud, no shared group exists, so aws genuinely needs the value in two
places — that is correct, not duplication to fix.)

## Verification (no cloud creds needed)

Fake an autogenerated inventory and resolve the var with a dummy vault pw:

  cd <repo>
  cat > inventories/hcloud_autogenerated.yml <<'YAML'
  all:
    hosts:
      zzhc: {ansible_host: 127.0.0.1}
  linux: {hosts: {zzhc: {}}}
  hcloud: {hosts: {zzhc: {}}}
  YAML
  cat > inventories/aws_autogenerated.yml <<'YAML'
  all:
    hosts:
      zzaws: {ansible_host: 127.0.0.1}
  linux: {hosts: {zzaws: {}}}
  aws: {hosts: {zzaws: {}}}
  YAML
  ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD=dummy ansible-inventory --host zzhc  2>/dev/null | grep admin_user  # -> "root"
  ANSIBLE_VAULT_PASSWORD=dummy ansible-inventory --host zzaws 2>/dev/null | grep admin_user  # -> "ubuntu"
  rm inventories/hcloud_autogenerated.yml inventories/aws_autogenerated.yml

Regression check (old dynamic hcloud host still resolves root via group
hcloud after the move): create a temp host in group `hcloud` only (NOT
hcloud_linux) and confirm admin_user resolves to root. The move keeps old
dynamic hcloud working because dynamic hosts are in BOTH hcloud and
hcloud_linux; the var now comes from hcloud.

## Constraints

- Constitution: Principle XI (DRY) drives approach B; Principle IV (YAGNI),
  Principle XII (Fail Loud) — keep behavior, do not add fallbacks.
- Commit via `commit` skill: fix: prefix, Co-authored-by Claude Code.
- Two logical changes (hcloud move, aws add) can be one commit (coherent:
  "configure cloud hosts via generated inventory") or two. Prefer one.
- Push to origin only (fork). Run bd export after close.

## Out of scope (file separate issue if pursued)

Retiring the dynamic inventory plugin files (hcloud.yml, aws_ec2.yml). Per
architecture discussion, generated inventory is the chosen direction, but the
create tasks still include_vars the *_linux group_vars for AMI/type/region, so
those files stay. Do NOT delete them here.

title: Add Hetzner Cloud (hcloud) provider to create-vm.yml/destroy-vm.yml
description: Implement spec specs/012-hcloud-vm-provider/spec.md: add provider=hcloud option to create-vm.yml and destroy-vm.yml, mirroring the tart/docker provider-dispatch pattern (specs/009, specs/011). Reuses hetzner.hcloud collection, HCLOUD_TOKEN, existing hcloud group_vars defaults. New hostname pool playbooks/vars/hostname_pool_hcloud.yml (10 LOTR place names) and inventories/hcloud_autogenerated.yml. Must not touch provision.yml/destroy.yml/hcloud-linux provisioning workflow or change tart/docker behavior.
design: SSH host-key policy is exposure-based, NOT uniform across providers. hcloud (internet-exposed) MUST verify the host key: ansible_ssh_common_args uses StrictHostKeyChecking=accept-new against a project-scoped UserKnownHostsFile (inventories/hcloud_known_hosts), NOT StrictHostKeyChecking=no + /dev/null. accept-new trusts a fresh server on first contact but refuses a CHANGED key (MITM / IP-reuse signal), failing loud (Principle XII). This deliberately diverges from tart/docker, which reach only laptop-local VMs (MITM requires prior host compromise) and therefore keep /dev/null. destroy-vm.yml removes the host key from the scoped file via ssh-keygen -f <file> -R <ip> (idempotent), preventing false REMOTE HOST IDENTIFICATION HAS CHANGED alarms on IP reassignment. known_hosts file is local state, gitignored like the autogenerated inventory. Rationale captured in an ADR.
notes: Code-complete and architect-approved: T001-T005,T007-T008,T010-T011 implemented and committed (f7398dd). 6 syntax-checks pass. Remaining work tracked in blocking issue ansible-all-my-things-c38d (T006/T009 real-cloud validation, needs HCLOUD_TOKEN).
close_reason: Shipped to main: hcloud provider implemented (f7398dd), real-cloud validation passed (c38d), T011 doc pass done

title: Docker hostname pool selection ignores pool order (non-deterministic across invocations)
description: docker.yml's hostname selection uses (hostname_pool | difference(used_hostnames)) | first. Ansible's difference filter is list(set(a)-set(b)), so element order depends on Python's per-process hash seed, not pool order. Observed: with an empty inventory, two consecutive create-vm.yml -e provider=docker invocations picked 'coruscant' then 'kamino' instead of 'tatooine' (pool order). spec.md lines 158/233 (FR-002/FR-011/data-model) require sequential, pool-order assignment ('the first name not yet in inventory, in pool order'). Found during e2e test of beads 9au2 (T021).
acceptance_criteria: hostname selection in playbooks/tasks/create/docker.yml deterministically picks the first hostname_pool entry not present in current_inventory.all.hosts, in pool-file order, regardless of process hash seed
close_reason: Verified e2e: rebuilt ansible-vm-docker:latest image (python3 + systemd-sysv), recreated container -> ansible ping pong, sshd active, hostname assigned 'tatooine' (pool-order, first unused entry with kamino consumed).

On Ubuntu, /sbin/init is provided by systemd-sysv (not systemd alone). Blocks T021 (quickstart e2e) and all US1/US2 manual validation steps that need a running container.
acceptance_criteria: Dockerfile installs systemd-sysv (or equivalent); docker run --privileged ... ansible-vm-docker:latest starts successfully and reaches a state where sshd is reachable
close_reason: Verified e2e: rebuilt ansible-vm-docker:latest image (python3 + systemd-sysv), recreated container -> ansible ping pong, sshd active, hostname assigned 'tatooine' (pool-order, first unused entry with kamino consumed).

title: Amend constitution Governance: scope rules-files history exemption, require specs/NNN-*/ stay current
description: The constitution's Governance section (final paragraph) exempts rules files (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, skill SKILL.md files, the constitution's own Sync Impact Report) from containing version history/changelogs/amendment logs, stating these files 'document current operational rules only' and git history is the canonical record of past reports. This exemption is currently scoped only to rules files but was informally over-extended (during review discussion) to specs/NNN-*/ directories, implying old feature specs could be left out of sync with renames in the live codebase. Amend the Governance section to add a clarifying sentence: 'This exemption applies only to the rules files named above. Feature specifications under specs/NNN-*/ are not exempt: they MUST stay consistent with the current codebase (file paths, names, interfaces) for any feature they describe, since they serve as the blueprint for recreating the system. Git history, not an outdated spec, is the record of how an implementation evolved.' This is a PATCH version bump (clarifying an existing rule's scope, no new principle) per the constitution's own versioning rules -- update the Sync Impact Report comment and Last Amended date accordingly.

title: Document podman provider as future Outlook idea in roadmap

Podman is comparable in size to the docker provider (reuses docker.yml's skeleton: podman build, podman run -d --privileged, podman port), but carries open risks: rootless vs rootful systemd-in-container behavior (cgroup v2 delegation, subuid/subgid mapping) under --privileged; 'podman port <name> 22/tcp' output format vs the regex used for docker_ssh_port; podman's native --systemd=true support possibly simplifying the cgroup setup vs docker's privileged-systemd approach; and the need for a new hostname pool file + 'podman' inventory group, mirroring the docker provider pattern. Decided as low priority for now -- no Linux box needed today for VM provisioning, and docker engine without docker desktop remains free. Recommend a short spike against a real podman host before estimating further, when this becomes relevant.

title: Add explanatory comment above 'Discover published SSH port' task in docker.yml

title: failed_when: false on Get VM running state masks tart list failures
description:
## Problem

The 'Get VM running state' task in playbooks/tasks/create/tart.yml uses failed_when: false. If tart list fails (e.g. tart not found, daemon error), the task silently succeeds with empty stdout. The subsequent when: vm_state.stdout | trim != 'running' condition then evaluates to true, causing tart set and tart run to execute against an unknown state — violating Principle XII (Fail Loud).

## What needs to be done

Remove failed_when: false and let the task fail naturally when tart list returns non-zero. Add a failed_when condition that catches genuine errors while still allowing the case where the VM is not found (rc == 0 but VM absent from list).

Note: jq is NOT a preferred replacement for the Python JSON parsing — Python3 is guaranteed on macOS, jq is an undeclared dependency. Keep the Python inline parse.

## Acceptance criteria
- tart list failure causes the task to fail with a clear error (Principle XII)
- Task still reports changed=false when tart list succeeds regardless of VM state
close_reason: Closed

title: Add vm_disk_size_gb variable to create-vm (default 45 GB)
description:
## Problem

Disk size is not configurable in the create-vm flow. The tart set call in playbooks/tasks/create/tart.yml only sets --cpu and --memory; the VM inherits whatever size the OCI image ships with and there is no way to override it.

## What needs to be done

1. Add vm_disk_size_gb variable to playbooks/create-vm.yml (default: 45)
2. Append --disk-size {{ vm_disk_size_gb }} to the tart set command in playbooks/tasks/create/tart.yml (line 94)

## Acceptance criteria
- create-vm.yml exposes vm_disk_size_gb with default 45
- tart set passes --disk-size to the VM
- Idempotent re-runs preserve the disk size (tart set is only called when VM is stopped)
close_reason: Closed

title: Replace wait_for port:22 with wait_for_connection in create-vm tart task
description:
## Problem

wait_for port: 22 with delay: 5 hangs for >1 minute on first VM boot even when SSH is already accepting connections (confirmed by manual ssh from host).

Root cause: tart's virtual network (macOS Virtualization.framework) causes Python socket.connect() in Ansible's wait_for to time out per attempt (default connect_timeout: 5s) while the ssh binary succeeds on the same connection. ~60 failed 5s-timeout cycles accumulate before any success.

## Fix

Replace the wait_for task in playbooks/tasks/create/tart.yml (line 124) with wait_for_connection delegated to the VM IP:

```yaml
- name: Wait for SSH on VM
  ansible.builtin.wait_for_connection:
    timeout: 300
    delay: 5
  delegate_to: "{{ vm_ip }}"
  vars:
    ansible_user: admin
    ansible_ssh_pass: "{{ tart_admin_password }}"
    ansible_ssh_common_args: "-o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null"
```

wait_for_connection performs a real SSH handshake (same code path as the ssh binary), so it cannot false-negative the way a raw TCP socket check does on macOS virtual networks.

## Acceptance criteria
- create-vm completes without hanging at the SSH wait step on first boot
- wait_for_connection returns within a few seconds when SSH is already accepting
close_reason: Closed

title: Findings gate: review of 6r6j (replace vagrant with tart CLI)

title: Replace vagrant with direct tart CLI in create-vm flow
description:
The spike (r5vs) confirmed that vagrant-tart's cloud-init injection is the root cause of the 17-min boot delay. Bare tart (no vagrant) boots SSH-ready in 6-7s — 140x faster than baseline, 43x inside the 300s target.

## What needs to be done

Replace vagrant in playbooks/tasks/create/tart.yml with direct tart CLI calls:
- tart clone <image> <name> (idempotency via creates: on the VM dir)
- tart run <name> --no-graphics
- tart ip <name> poll + wait_for port 22 (already in place)
- Rescue block (tart.yml:161-178) reimplemented as: tart stop, tart delete, VM-dir removal, fail loud

Primary implementation decision: SSH credentials. vagrant-tart currently injects the admin password via cloud-init (nocloud datasource). Without vagrant, an alternative is required — either inject a cloud-init user-data seed before first run, or use an SSH key pre-provisioned on the OCI image.

## Acceptance criteria
- vagrant up replaced by tart clone + tart run in create-vm task file
- Rescue/cleanup/fail-loud guarantees preserved (tart stop + tart delete + dir-remove + fail)
- SSH credentials solved (admin user reachable after boot)
- create-vm playbook end-to-end completes in under 300s
- Idempotent re-runs work correctly
close_reason: Closed

title: Warn instead of fail in destroy-vm when hostname missing from inventory

Change the assert to a warning debug task so destroy can proceed with vagrant destroy -f and tart cleanup even when the inventory entry is absent.

title: Add rescue block to create-vm tart tasks to roll back on failure
description: create/tart.yml has no rollback. When vagrant up fails (observed: communicator=none error), the tart VM is already running but the playbook never reaches the inventory-write step, leaving a ghost VM: exists in tart, Vagrantfile on disk, no inventory entry. Add a block/rescue around the vagrant up + tart ip + wait_for + inventory-write tasks. On rescue: run vagrant destroy -f, remove the VM directory, and fail with a clear message. Re-running create-vm.yml should then work cleanly.
close_reason: block/rescue wraps all post-directory tasks; rescue: vagrant destroy -f + remove dir + fail with message

title: Fix destroy-vm: load autogenerated inventory conditionally like create-vm does

VM teardown (vagrant destroy, tart delete) must still proceed even when the inventory file is absent.
close_reason: Mirrored create/tart.yml stat+conditional-load guard in destroy/tart.yml; assert order corrected

title: Fix: communicator=none unsupported by installed vagrant-tart — vagrant up fails
description: vagrant up in create-vm.yml fails with 'The requested communicator none could not be found' because the installed vagrant-tart version does not support communicator=none. The VM starts successfully (clone, configure, start all pass) but vagrant aborts when initialising the communicator. Fix: either upgrade vagrant-tart to a version that supports communicator=none, or replace the approach with one that does not require it (e.g. boot_timeout or skipping SSH config).
close_reason: Replaced communicator=none with SSH credentials + boot_timeout=0 in Vagrantfile.j2

title: Fix vagrant up SSH timeout: use communicator=none and poll SSH from Ansible
description:
vagrant up fails with SSH boot timeout when creating a Tart VM. The VM starts successfully but Vagrant's SSH wait times out before the network bridge is ready (observed: 5m06s elapsed, admin/admin SSH works after the fact).

Fix: set config.vm.communicator = 'none' in the Vagrantfile template so vagrant up does not wait for SSH. After vagrant up, retrieve the VM IP via 'tart ip <hostname>' and poll port 22 with ansible.builtin.wait_for before writing the inventory entry. This matches the pattern used in cloud VM provisioning playbooks.
close_reason: Implemented: communicator=none in Vagrantfile.j2, tart ip retry loop + wait_for SSH in tart.yml, vault credentials file with TODO to encrypt
